_meta.source: 15 CFR Part 740 (Country Groups), 15 CFR Part 746 (Embargoes), 15 CFR Part 742 (Control Policy), BIS Country Chart
_meta.citation: 15 CFR Part 740 Supp. 1 (Country Groups); 15 CFR § 746.2 (Cuba); § 746.3 (Iraq); § 746.4 (Iran); § 746.5 (North Korea); § 746.6 (Syria); § 746.7 (Russia/Belarus); § 746.9 (China advanced computing)
_meta.retrieved_by: Manual seed — run scripts/refresh_rules.py to update
_meta.notes: PLACEHOLDER — country group membership and restriction details must be verified from the current BIS country chart and CFR. This file covers only a policy-relevant subset of countries; it is not an exhaustive list.

A.description: Allied and partner nations — generally favoured treatment under many EAR provisions.
A.note: Full list in Supplement No. 1 to Part 740. Subgroups A:1 through A:6 have specific meanings.

B.description: Most countries in the world — standard EAR treatment applies.
B.note: Default group. Items controlled AT1 may require licence for some Country B destinations.

D:1.description: National Security (NS) controlled countries — heightened scrutiny for NS1-controlled items.
D:1.note: NS1 column on BIS Country Chart. Exporting 5D002, 5A002, 3A090, 4A090 to D:1 countries generally requires a licence.

D:5.description: US arms-embargoed countries — most items controlled MT, NS, AT, or CC require licence.

E:1.description: Terrorist-supporting countries — comprehensive restrictions; most EAR licence exceptions unavailable.
E:1.note: Iran, Syria, North Korea, Cuba. OFAC also maintains comprehensive or targeted sanctions on these countries.

E:2.description: Unilaterally controlled countries for additional EAR restrictions.
E:2.note: Russia and Belarus added under § 746.8 restrictions effective March 2022.

CN.name: China (People's Republic of China)
CN.advanced_computing_citation: 15 CFR § 746.9; BIS Final Rule Oct. 7, 2022 and subsequent amendments
CN.notes: China is in Country Groups D:1 and D:5, but not E:1. However, § 746.9 imposes chip-specific controls comparable to embargo-level restrictions for 3A090 and 4A090 items. OFAC does not maintain a comprehensive China embargo but has SDN designations for specific entities. Deemed-export concerns are acute for Chinese nationals accessing advanced computing or AI items.
CN.ofac_program: None (comprehensive); targeted SDN/Entity List entries apply
CN.entity_list_concentration: High — numerous Chinese semiconductor, AI, and telecom firms are on BIS Entity List

RU.name: Russia (Russian Federation)
part746_restrictions: § 746.8 (Belarus — applies to Russia by cross-reference for dual export)
RU.notes: Russia: extensive BIS export controls under § 746.7 following Feb. 2022 invasion of Ukraine. Most dual-use items require a licence, and most exceptions are suspended. OFAC maintains broad sectoral sanctions on Russian financial, energy, and defence sectors.
RU.oil_energy_notes: OFAC energy-sector sanctions under Directive 4 (post-Feb 2022) restrict engagement with Russian energy sector entities. EAR energy sector controls under § 746.7(a)(3).

BY.name: Belarus
BY.notes: Belarus: BIS controls under § 746.8, mirroring § 746.7 Russia controls. OFAC sanctions on Belarusian government and oligarchs. Re-export via Belarus to Russia is a key enforcement concern.

IR.name: Iran (Islamic Republic of Iran)
IR.notes: Iran: comprehensive OFAC embargo (ITSR — Iranian Transactions and Sanctions Regulations, 31 CFR Part 560). BIS controls under § 746.4. Virtually all exports/reexports require a licence, with very limited exceptions. High enforcement priority.
IR.ofac_program: IRAN (ITSR — 31 CFR Part 560), ISA, NSR, NPWMD and others

KP.name: North Korea (Democratic People's Republic of Korea)
KP.notes: North Korea: comprehensive embargo. BIS § 746.5 restricts virtually all exports. OFAC North Korea Sanctions Regulations (31 CFR Part 510). UN Security Council resolutions impose multilateral restrictions.

CU.name: Cuba
CU.notes: Cuba: comprehensive embargo under BIS § 746.2 and OFAC Cuban Assets Control Regulations (31 CFR Part 515). Note: US policy on Cuba has been subject to executive-level adjustments; verify current status.

SY.name: Syria (Syrian Arab Republic)
SY.notes: Syria: comprehensive BIS restrictions under § 746.6. OFAC Syrian Sanctions Regulations (31 CFR Part 542). Note: political situation evolving post-2024; verify current OFAC/BIS status for any specific engagement.

VE.name: Venezuela
VE.notes: Venezuela: targeted (not comprehensive) US sanctions. OFAC EO 13808, 13827, 13835, 13850 targeting Venezuelan government, oil sector (PDVSA), and gold sector. BIS Entity List entries for some Venezuelan entities. Not a BIS § 746 comprehensive embargo country, but high-risk due to OFAC oil-sector restrictions.
